scenarios.title: Late Shift Swap
scenarios.setting: Two lab coordinators repeatedly decide whether to cover each other's late equipment checks.
scenarios.dilemma: Covering the other person protects the project, but refusing preserves personal time if the other still helps.

agents.goal: Keep the study schedule on track without burning out.
agents.risk: Being seen as always available for extra work.

agents.goal: Prove reliability while protecting family obligations.
agents.risk: Losing trust if they decline too often.

scenarios.cooperate_action: cover the check and document what happened
scenarios.defect_action: leave the check for the other coordinator

scenarios.title: Roommate Sink Reset
scenarios.setting: Roommates face a nightly choice about cleaning shared dishes after dinner.
scenarios.dilemma: Cleaning helps everyone, but skipping is tempting when the other roommate may clean anyway.

agents.goal: Wake up to a usable kitchen.
Avoid chores after exhausting shifts.

scenarios.cooperate_action: wash a fair share before bed
scenarios.defect_action: leave dishes in the sink

scenarios.title: Open Source Review Queue
scenarios.setting: Two maintainers alternate between reviewing each other's pull requests.
scenarios.dilemma: Timely review keeps momentum, but each maintainer can free ride on the other's review labor.

agents.goal: Keep releases moving without doing all review work.
agents.risk: Approving rushed changes that create bugs.

agents.goal: Get docs fixes merged while helping code quality.
agents.risk: Review work crowding out writing time.

scenarios.cooperate_action: review promptly with actionable notes
scenarios.defect_action: delay review and ask the other maintainer to handle it

scenarios.title: Shared Sidewalk Snow
scenarios.setting: Neighbors decide after each snowfall whether to clear the shared sidewalk edge.
scenarios.dilemma: Clearing the sidewalk helps both households, but each can wait for the other to shovel.

agents.goal: Keep the block passable for elders and deliveries.
agents.risk: Taking on an unfair winter workload.

agents.goal: Save time before commuting.
agents.risk: A fall or citation if no one clears the path.

scenarios.cooperate_action: shovel the shared stretch
scenarios.defect_action: wait and hope the neighbor shovels

scenarios.title: Annotation Quality Loop
scenarios.setting: Two analysts repeatedly decide whether to double-check labels in a shared dataset.
scenarios.dilemma: Careful checking improves the dataset, but skipping saves time if the partner catches mistakes.

agents.goal: Deliver accurate labels before a model run.
agents.risk: Missing subtle errors under deadline pressure.

agents.goal: Finish the batch while preserving label consistency.

scenarios.cooperate_action: audit labels and flag uncertainties
scenarios.defect_action: accept labels without checking

scenarios.title: Clinic Supply Pool
scenarios.setting: A small clinic asks teams to contribute supplies to a shared emergency cabinet.
scenarios.dilemma: Contributing protects all patients, but each team is tempted to keep supplies for its own room.

agents.goal: Guarantee rapid access to essentials.
Protect child-specific supplies.
agents.risk: Contributing items that are hard to restock.

agents.goal: Reduce delays for walk-in patients.
agents.risk: Being the only team that contributes.

scenarios.cooperate_action: add supplies to the shared cabinet
scenarios.defect_action: hold supplies back for the home team

scenarios.title: Study Group Notes
scenarios.setting: Students decide whether to contribute high-quality notes to a shared exam folder.
scenarios.dilemma: Shared notes help everyone learn, but each student can download the folder without adding work.

agents.goal: Build a complete study guide.
agents.risk: Doing unpaid tutoring for the group.

agents.goal: Get concise review material.
Understand missed classes.

scenarios.cooperate_action: upload clear notes with corrections
scenarios.defect_action: download notes without contributing

scenarios.title: Block Watch Rotation
scenarios.setting: Residents coordinate evening walks after a series of package thefts.
scenarios.dilemma: Walking the block deters theft for everyone, but individual households would rather stay home.

agents.goal: Create a fair rotation that improves safety.
Protect family routines and deliveries.
Feel safe without taking unsafe walks.

scenarios.cooperate_action: take an assigned watch shift or arrange coverage
scenarios.defect_action: skip the rotation without notice

scenarios.title: Model Card Maintenance
scenarios.setting: Teams using a shared model decide whether to update its model card after each deployment.
scenarios.dilemma: Updates help every downstream user, but teams under deadline can benefit from others documenting risks.

agents.goal: Keep model documentation accurate.
Ship features with clear limitations.
Track safety-relevant changes.

scenarios.cooperate_action: contribute deployment notes and known limitations
scenarios.defect_action: ship without updating the shared card

scenarios.title: Mutual Aid Fridge
scenarios.setting: Volunteers decide whether to restock and clean a neighborhood community fridge.
scenarios.dilemma: Regular care keeps the fridge useful, but each volunteer can rely on others to do the upkeep.

agents.goal: Keep fresh food available.
Keep the fridge safe and dignified.
Make sure neighbors know the fridge exists.

scenarios.cooperate_action: complete a restock, cleaning, or outreach task
scenarios.defect_action: skip upkeep while still taking credit

scenarios.title: Parent Teacher Support Plan
scenarios.setting: A parent and teacher negotiate a support plan for a student who is falling behind.
scenarios.dilemma: Both want the student to recover, but each worries about taking on an unsustainable share of the plan.

agents.goal: Create a realistic academic support routine.
agents.risk: Accepting responsibilities that exceed classroom capacity.

agents.goal: Secure help without shaming the student.

scenarios.cooperate_action: offer a concrete concession and ask for constraints
scenarios.defect_action: shift blame and demand unilateral effort

scenarios.title: Deadline Reset
scenarios.setting: A product manager and engineer renegotiate scope after a late-breaking bug.
scenarios.dilemma: They can jointly protect quality by changing scope, or each can defend their own performance metrics.

agents.goal: Preserve customer commitments.
Ship reliable code without heroics.

scenarios.cooperate_action: trade scope for quality with transparent rationale
scenarios.defect_action: protect one metric and externalize the cost

scenarios.title: Apartment Repair Schedule
scenarios.setting: A tenant and landlord negotiate repairs after repeated heating failures.
scenarios.dilemma: They can coordinate repairs and temporary accommodations, or each can escalate defensively.

agents.goal: Get heat restored quickly and safely.
Resolve the issue without unnecessary cost.
agents.risk: Accepting liability for broader building problems.

scenarios.cooperate_action: share constraints and commit to a repair step
scenarios.defect_action: deny responsibility and threaten escalation

scenarios.title: Research Credit Order
scenarios.setting: Collaborators negotiate authorship order after uneven contributions to a paper.
scenarios.dilemma: They can make contribution evidence explicit, or compete for credit through selective memory.

agents.goal: Receive credit needed for career progress.
Have invisible coordination work recognized.
agents.risk: Being squeezed out of first authorship.

agents.goal: Keep the collaboration intact and accurate.

scenarios.cooperate_action: name evidence and propose a transparent credit rule
scenarios.defect_action: argue from status and minimize others' work

scenarios.title: Holiday Shift Coverage
scenarios.setting: Coworkers negotiate who will cover a holiday shift at a busy service desk.
scenarios.dilemma: They can share constraints and rotate fairly, or hide preferences and pressure the most flexible person.

agents.goal: Spend the holiday with family.
Keep the job secure while asking for fairness.
Keep the desk staffed with minimal resentment.
agents.risk: Setting a precedent that feels arbitrary.

scenarios.cooperate_action: offer a fair rotation or compensating trade
scenarios.defect_action: withhold constraints and push the cost onto someone else

scenarios.title: Moderation Apology
scenarios.setting: A community moderator repairs trust after mistakenly removing a member's post.
scenarios.dilemma: The moderator can acknowledge harm and clarify policy, or defend the decision to preserve authority.

agents.goal: Restore trust while keeping moderation standards clear.
Have the mistake acknowledged and corrected.

scenarios.cooperate_action: acknowledge impact and propose a repair step
scenarios.defect_action: focus on authority and dismiss the concern

scenarios.title: Cross-Cultural Meeting Repair
scenarios.setting: Team members repair a meeting after one person's direct feedback was interpreted as disrespect.
scenarios.dilemma: They can separate intent from impact and reset norms, or argue about who is too sensitive.

agents.goal: Keep feedback efficient and honest.
Maintain dignity and psychological safety.
Reset norms without blaming either person.

scenarios.cooperate_action: reflect impact and propose a norm for next time
scenarios.defect_action: argue about intent and question motives

scenarios.title: Misread Family Text
scenarios.setting: Siblings repair a conflict after a short text message sounded cold during a stressful week.
scenarios.dilemma: They can ask what was meant and name feelings, or retaliate with silence and sarcasm.

agents.goal: Explain the context without being defensive.
agents.risk: Having every brief message treated as hostile.

agents.goal: Feel respected and included.
agents.risk: Escalating based on a mistaken read.

scenarios.cooperate_action: ask for clarification and validate the feeling
scenarios.defect_action: assume bad intent and withdraw

scenarios.title: Delayed Refund Repair
scenarios.setting: A support agent and customer discuss a delayed refund that has already missed one promised date.
scenarios.dilemma: They can stay truthful and repair trust, or trade blame and threats.

agents.goal: Resolve the refund without making false promises.
agents.risk: Being blamed for a back-office delay.

agents.goal: Get a reliable update and fair remedy.

scenarios.cooperate_action: state what is known, apologize, and offer a concrete next step
scenarios.defect_action: make vague promises or accuse the other side

scenarios.title: Public Health Thread
scenarios.setting: A public health communicator replies to a skeptical resident in a local forum.
scenarios.dilemma: They can answer with evidence and respect, or dismiss the resident and deepen distrust.

agents.goal: Correct misinformation without humiliating the resident.
Have safety concerns taken seriously.
agents.risk: Rejecting reliable evidence out of distrust.

scenarios.cooperate_action: answer the concern with evidence and respect
scenarios.defect_action: mock the concern or spread unsupported claims

scenarios.title: Disaster Generator Allocation
scenarios.setting: Emergency coordinators allocate a limited set of generators after a storm.
scenarios.dilemma: They can allocate by transparent need, or favor visible constituencies and leave quieter groups exposed.

agents.goal: Keep critical care powered.
Protect residents without power.
agents.risk: Being deprioritized because needs are less visible.

agents.goal: Make a defensible allocation under pressure.
agents.risk: Political backlash from any unmet group.

scenarios.cooperate_action: share need data and accept a transparent allocation rule
scenarios.defect_action: inflate claims or lobby for a favored group

scenarios.title: Shared GPU Queue
scenarios.setting: Research groups allocate limited GPU time before a grant deadline.
scenarios.dilemma: They can reveal true urgency and share slots, or exaggerate priority and starve other projects.

agents.goal: Finish a benchmark run before submission.
Run ablations needed for a paper.
Allocate compute transparently.

scenarios.cooperate_action: state real deadlines and accept queue limits
scenarios.defect_action: overstate urgency and hoard GPU slots

scenarios.title: Food Bank Distribution
scenarios.setting: Partner organizations allocate a short shipment of staple foods.
scenarios.dilemma: They can match food to documented need, or compete for inventory based on institutional influence.

agents.goal: Serve a high daily visitor count.
Reach isolated households with fewer alternatives.
agents.risk: Being overlooked due to lower volume.

agents.goal: Allocate fairly across different need profiles.
agents.risk: Using a metric that misses hidden hardship.

scenarios.cooperate_action: share need evidence and support a proportional split
scenarios.defect_action: claim extra inventory without evidence

scenarios.title: Family Care Schedule
scenarios.setting: Adult siblings allocate caregiving time for a parent recovering from surgery.
scenarios.dilemma: They can share constraints honestly, or hide availability and let one sibling absorb the burden.

agents.goal: Support the parent without becoming default caregiver.
agents.risk: Having proximity treated as unlimited availability.

agents.goal: Contribute meaningfully despite travel limits.
Receive steady care without causing conflict.

scenarios.cooperate_action: name constraints and take a fair care slot
scenarios.defect_action: avoid commitments while criticizing the plan

scenarios.title: Classroom Device Cart
scenarios.setting: Teachers allocate a limited cart of tablets for lessons during testing week.
scenarios.dilemma: They can schedule based on learning need, or reserve extra devices to protect their own lesson plans.

agents.goal: Run a lab simulation that needs tablets.
Support students with translation tools.
agents.risk: Having accessibility needs treated as optional.

agents.goal: Create a fair schedule and prevent conflicts.
agents.risk: Being pulled into every classroom dispute.

scenarios.cooperate_action: share device needs and accept a transparent schedule
scenarios.defect_action: reserve more devices than needed
